QA Bug Analysis — Grimbledee’s Field Report (Team Biscuit)

This document captures the bugs reported by Grimbledee during testing of the Roll20 GeneSeed suite, along with analysis and suggested fixes.

First: thank you. Grimbledee’s testing is the good kind—clear observations, practical repro paths, and the kind of notes that actually move needles. Team Biscuit did us a real service here.

Notes from the Librarian:

  • This report aims to isolate root causes and restore expected player flows.
  • In general, we prefer loud, diagnosable failures over quiet degradation.

How to read this (Vivian’s margin notes):

  • Look for the Description for what the player sees.
  • Look for the Working Theory / Root Cause for why it’s happening.
  • Look for Suggested Approach for what we do next.
  • If you see a blockquote labeled Vivian, that’s me translating “code pain” into “human words.”

1. HTML Encodings Issue

Description

Text in the UI is displaying HTML entity codes like &amp;, &lt;, &gt; instead of their intended symbols (&, <, >).

Vivian: This is what happens when we “sanitize” the same string twice—like polishing a mirror until you can only see the cloth.

Analysis

Location: Multiple files implement their own escapeHtml() functions with inconsistent behavior:

  • Cauldron.js: Contains a non-functional version that only converts to string without escaping
  • PurseStrings.js: Has two definitions — one that properly replaces special characters, but double-encoding may be happening
  • PlayerSoul.js: Uses escapeHtml() throughout the UI rendering (lines 6239, 6241, 6255, etc.)

Root Cause: The problem appears to be double-encoding or decode/encode cycle issues. When text is encoded multiple times (e.g., & → &amp; → &amp;amp;), or when already-encoded text is displayed raw, users see the entity codes instead of symbols.

Affected Areas:

  • Vendor UIs (PurseStrings)
  • PlayerSoul dashboard panels
  • Any HTML output using the inconsistent escapeHtml functions

Suggested Approach

  1. Audit all escapeHtml() function definitions across the codebase
  2. Standardize on a single, canonical implementation (likely in CoreDNA.js or CoreStyles.js)
  3. Add a corresponding unescapeHtml() or decodeHtml() function for reading stored data
  4. Ensure data is encoded exactly once when storing and decoded when reading
  5. Check for double-encoding in vendor/shop HTML generation

2. Number of Items Available for Sale at Vendors

Description

Issue with how the number of items available for sale is displayed or tracked at vendors.

Vivian: If the shelf says “3 potions” but the cupboard says “2,” someone’s going to accuse us of sorcery. Counts must match reality, or the UI loses trust.

Analysis

Location:

  • PurseStrings.js: Vendor/shop commerce system
  • NpcSoul.js: NPC vendor implementations
  • Related TODO item exists at /TODO/npcsoul-vendor-implementation.md

Current Understanding: The issue description is vague but could relate to:

  • Incorrect inventory counts being displayed
  • Items showing as available when they're not
  • Stock quantities not updating after purchase
  • Display formatting issues in vendor panels

Affected Code:

  • Vendor inventory management in PurseStrings
  • Shop UI rendering in PlayerSoul panels
  • NPC vendor inventory tracking in NpcSoul

Suggested Approach

  1. Review the vendor inventory display logic in PurseStrings
  2. Check if inventory counts are properly decremented after purchases
  3. Verify the NpcSoul vendor implementation matches expected behavior
  4. Add logging to track inventory state changes during purchases
  5. Review the TODO document for known vendor implementation gaps

3. Vendor Filter Reset on Purchase

Description

When buying from a vendor with a filter applied (e.g., "consumables" filter), completing the purchase resets the filter back to "All" instead of maintaining the active filter.

Vivian: This is the UI equivalent of a shopkeeper shouting “NEW CUSTOMER!” and rearranging the entire store mid-transaction. Keep the filter.

Analysis

Location: PurseStrings.js — vendor buy/sell transaction handlers

Root Cause: The purchase transaction likely rebuilds or refreshes the vendor UI without preserving the current filter state. The filter is probably stored in a local variable or UI state that gets cleared when the panel is regenerated after a successful purchase.

Expected Behavior: After purchasing an item, the vendor panel should:

  1. Update the inventory/stock counts
  2. Refresh the displayed items
  3. Maintain the currently active filter setting
  4. Keep the user's view context (scroll position, filter, etc.)

Affected Flow:

User applies filter → Views filtered items → Clicks buy → Transaction completes →
Panel refreshes → Filter state lost → Shows "All" items

Suggested Approach

  1. Add filter state persistence to the vendor panel state
  2. Pass current filter as a parameter when regenerating the UI after purchase
  3. Consider using CoreDNA state management to persist filter selection
  4. Add filter state to the URL/command parameters for the vendor panel refresh
  5. Test that filter survives both successful and failed purchase attempts

4. Consume Button Text Color Inconsistency

Description

In the Overview panel's Consume section, button text colors are inconsistent:

  • Eat(1): GREEN when food available ✓ (correct)
  • Drink(0): GRAY when nothing to drink ✓ (correct)
  • Booze(1): WHITE when booze available ✗ (should be green)
  • Potion(2): WHITE when potions available ✗ (should be green)

Vivian: Color is language. Green says “go ahead, darling.” White says “I’m decorative.” These should not be ambiguous when the action is available.

Analysis

Location: PlayerSoul.js lines 6237-6248

Code Examination:

const linkOrDisabled = (label, href, enabled, primary) => {
    if (!enabled) {
        return `<span ${primary ? actionBtnPrimaryDisabledAttr : actionBtnDisabledAttr}>${escapeHtml(label)}</span>`;
    }
    return `<a ${primary ? actionBtnPrimaryAttr : actionBtnAttr} href="${escapeAttr(href)}">${escapeHtml(label)}</a>`;
};
const consumeButtons = [
    linkOrDisabled(`Eat(${allFood.length})`, eatHref, allFood.length > 0, true),      // primary=true ✓
    linkOrDisabled(`Drink(${allDrink.length})`, drinkHref, allDrink.length > 0, false),  // primary=false
    linkOrDisabled(`Booze(${allBooze.length})`, boozeHref, allBooze.length > 0, false),  // primary=false ✗
    linkOrDisabled(`Potion(${allPotions.length})`, potionHref, allPotions.length > 0, false), // primary=false ✗
    linkOrDisabled('Rest', restHref, true, false)
];

Root Cause: The primary parameter (4th argument) is set to false for Booze and Potion buttons. When items are available, they should use the primary (green) styling to indicate availability, matching the Eat button behavior.

Impact: This inconsistency confuses users about which actions are available. All enabled consume buttons should have the same visual treatment.

Suggested Approach

Simple Fix: Change lines 6246-6247 to:

linkOrDisabled(`Booze(${allBooze.length})`, boozeHref, allBooze.length > 0, true),   // primary=true
linkOrDisabled(`Potion(${allPotions.length})`, potionHref, allPotions.length > 0, true), // primary=true

This makes all consume buttons use primary (green) styling when items are available, creating visual consistency across the interface.

5. Rod Break Message — Incomplete Sentence

Description

Fishing rod break message shows: "Your rod snapped while fighting a large." — sentence appears incomplete.

Vivian: “A large” what? A large fish? A large problem? A large existential dread? Give the noun back.

Analysis

Location: GoFish!.js line 1513

Current Code:

entry.stats.heroHtml = `<div style="padding:8px; border:1px solid #8a2c2c; border-radius:8px; background:#3a1f1f; color:#ffd1d1;"><strong>💥 Rod Break</strong><div style="font-size:12px; margin-top:4px;">Your rod snapped while fighting a ${data.fishSize || 'fish'}.</div></div>`;

Root Cause: The template uses data.fishSize which might contain just an adjective like "large" instead of a complete noun phrase like "large fish". When fishSize is a descriptor word, the sentence reads "...fighting a large." instead of "...fighting a large fish."

Expected: "Your rod snapped while fighting a large fish."

Suggested Approach

Option 1 — Simple Fix: Always append "fish" to ensure grammatical completeness:

`Your rod snapped while fighting a ${data.fishSize || 'large'} fish.`

Option 2 — Context-Aware: Check if fishSize is a complete phrase or just an adjective:

const fishDescription = data.fishSize && data.fishSize.includes('fish')
    ? data.fishSize
    : `${data.fishSize || 'large'} fish`;
`Your rod snapped while fighting a ${fishDescription}.`

Option 3 — Use Fish Name: If available, use the actual fish name:

`Your rod snapped while fighting a ${data.fishName || 'large fish'}.`

6. Fishing Leaderboard — "Go Fish" Button Does Nothing

Description

From the Fishing Leaderboard panel in PlayerSoul, clicking the "Go Fish" pill plays the fishing sound but no visible action occurs.

Vivian: Ah, the classic “the sound played, so surely it worked” illusion. If nothing changes on-screen and no error appears, the player assumes we’re ignoring them.

Analysis

Location: GoFish!.js lines 1599, 4567-4629

Button HTML (line 1599):

`<a style="${goFishBtnStyle}" href="!go-fish refish --charid|${charId} --poolid|${poolId}">
    <span>${goFishIcon}</span><span>${goFishLabel}</span>
</a>`

Command Handler (lines 4567-4629):

} else if (actionCommand === 'refish') {
    // ... validation code ...
    const refishToken = findObjs({ _type: 'graphic', represents: cid, layer: 'objects' })[0];
    if (!refishToken) {
        throw new Error("[Invalid] No active token found for refish.");
    }
    // ... more code ...
    GoFish.Panel.StartSessionForTokenWithMeta(refishToken, `Fishing at ${arg1}/${arg2}`, { poolId, source: arg1, level: arg2 }, { activate: true, quiet: true });
    fisher.goFishing(refishToken, arg1, arg2, toWhom);
}

Root Cause Theories:

  1. Token Not Found: The findObjs call for the token might fail if the character token isn't on the objects layer or is on a different page
  2. Silent Failure: The { quiet: true } option might suppress error messages, causing failures to be invisible
  3. Sound Plays But Logic Fails: Sound effect triggers but the actual fishing session fails to start
  4. Panel Not Refreshing: The fishing UI panel doesn't update to show the new session
  5. Missing Panel Context: Leaderboard view might not have the necessary context to start a new fishing session

Suggested Approach

  1. Add comprehensive logging to the refish command handler
  2. Remove or conditionally use { quiet: true } to allow error messages during debugging
  3. Validate token existence and state before attempting to fish
  4. Check if GoFish.Panel.RefreshIfVisible() is being called after the fishing action
  5. Verify that the leaderboard has access to the required poolId and location data
  6. Add user-facing error messages when fishing cannot start from the leaderboard
  7. Consider if the leaderboard should redirect to the main fishing panel instead

7. Eating Does Not Eat

Description

The eating functionality is not working — consuming food items does not actually consume them.

Vivian: If you click “Eat” and your inventory stays the same, the player’s brain concludes: “This system is pretend.” We need a visible, irreversible state change (or a loud failure).

Analysis

Location: PlayerSoul.js function consumeItem starting at line 9545

Current Implementation:
The consumeItem function is extensive (lines 9545-9900+) and handles:

  • Item lookup via PurseStrings.getCheckObjectByName(consumeable) (line 9550)
  • Parsing item modifiers (protien, hydration, buffs, etc.) starting at line 9599
  • Applying effects to the soul object (lines 9600-9800+)

Problem Areas:

  1. Item Removal: Need to verify that items are actually removed from inventory after consumption
  2. Transaction Commit: Changes might not be persisted to state
  3. Observer Notification: The notifyObservers('soulChange', soul, deepCopy(soul)) might not be triggered
  4. PurseStrings Integration: The item might not be removed from the character's inventory in the PurseStrings system
  5. Error Handling: Silent failures might prevent consumption without user feedback

Expected Flow:

User clicks "Eat" → Select item → consumeItem called →
Parse item effects → Apply to soul → Remove from inventory →
Update UI → Notify observers → Show success message

Suggested Approach

  1. Add detailed logging at each step of the consumeItem function
  2. Verify that PurseStrings.removeItemFromInventory() or equivalent is being called
  3. Check that changes to the soul object are being saved to state
  4. Ensure notifyObservers is called after successful consumption
  5. Verify the item query/dropdown is passing the correct item name
  6. Check for case-sensitivity or name-matching issues in item lookup
  7. Add user feedback (success/failure messages) at the end of consumption
  8. Test with A5edDB.GetDB() validation to ensure items are valid

8. Resting Does Not Rest

Description

Long rest functionality is not working — taking a long rest does not update the character's rest status.

Vivian: Rest is a contract: time passes, resources return, status updates. When that contract breaks, every other system feels suspect.

Analysis

Location: PlayerSoul.js function consumeRest starting at line 9189

Current Implementation:

function consumeRest(soul, message, restType) {
    // ... validation and setup ...

    switch (restType) {
        case LONG_REST_COMMAND:
            if (soul.insomnia) {
                // Block rest if insomnia
                return;
            }
            applyRestToSoul(RestInStyle.LongRest);  // <-- Delegates to RestInStyle
            soul.fatigue = FULLY_RESTED;             // <-- Updates soul state
            // ... fishing rod repair code ...
            notifyObservers('soulChange', soul, deepCopy(soul));  // <-- Notify
            // ... more code ...
    }
}

Integration Points:

  • Uses 5E Resting in Style.js via RestInStyle.LongRest (line 9227)
  • Updates soul.fatigue directly (line 9228)
  • Repairs fishing rods as a side effect (lines 9230-9248)
  • Calls notifyObservers to propagate changes (line 9249)

Problem Areas:

  1. RestInStyle Undefined: The RestInStyle script might not be loaded or the function might not exist
  2. Character Selection: message.selected might not contain the correct token reference
  3. State Not Persisting: Changes to soul.fatigue might not be saved back to state
  4. Observer Not Triggering: UI might not refresh after rest completes
  5. Error Swallowing: Multiple try/catch blocks with // ignore comments might hide critical errors

Expected Behavior:

  • Character HP restored
  • Hit dice reset/available
  • Spell slots restored
  • soul.fatigue set to FULLY_RESTED (1440)
  • Fishing rod durability restored to max
  • UI updated to show rested state

Suggested Approach

  1. Verify 5E Resting in Style.js is loaded and RestInStyle.LongRest exists
  2. Add error logging instead of silently ignoring exceptions
  3. Check that message.selected contains valid token objects
  4. Verify state changes are persisted via CoreDNA state management
  5. Ensure observer notifications trigger UI refresh
  6. Test with and without tokens selected
  7. Add explicit success/failure feedback to the user
  8. Check character sheet attribute updates (hp_current, spell slots, etc.)

9. Combat Panel Does Not Work

Description

The combat panel is not functioning properly.

Vivian: “Doesn’t work” is a mood, not a repro. We need: what you clicked, what you expected, what happened instead, and what the console whispered when it failed.

Analysis

Location:

  • Combat Master.js: Main combat system (276,569 bytes)
  • Mass Combat.js: Large-scale combat system (72,505 bytes)
  • RoundMaster.js: Turn tracking and round management (290,916 bytes)
  • PlayerSoul.js: May have combat panel UI integration

Insufficient Information: The bug report doesn't specify:

  • What "does not work" means (crashes, doesn't display, buttons don't work, etc.)
  • Which combat panel is affected (Combat Master, Mass Combat, or PlayerSoul combat UI)
  • What error messages appear, if any
  • What actions fail (starting combat, ending turns, tracking initiative, etc.)

Potential Issues:

  1. Script Load Order: Combat scripts might depend on other scripts not loaded yet
  2. Command Handler: Combat command parsing might be broken
  3. UI Rendering: Panel HTML generation might have errors
  4. State Management: Combat state might not be initializing properly
  5. Integration: Links from PlayerSoul to combat systems might be broken
  6. Dependencies: Missing required libraries or functions

Suggested Approach

  1. Gather More Information:

    • What specific action is attempted?
    • Are there any error messages in the API console?
    • Which combat system is being used?
    • Does the panel display at all, or fail to render?

  2. Debugging Steps:

    • Check the API console for JavaScript errors
    • Verify all combat scripts are loaded in correct order
    • Test basic commands like !combat help or equivalent
    • Check if CoreDNA and dependencies are loaded before combat scripts
    • Review script startup logs for initialization errors

  3. Common Combat Issues:

    • Token selection problems
    • Initiative tracking failures
    • Turn order display issues
    • Effect/status marker management
    • Integration with character sheets

  4. Next Steps:

    • Request detailed reproduction steps from Grimbledee
    • Collect API console logs during the failure
    • Test in isolation with minimal script load
    • Check for conflicts with other combat-related scripts

Summary of Findings

High Priority (Blocking User Actions)

  1. Eating Does Not Eat — Core functionality broken, prevents item consumption
  2. Resting Does Not Rest — Core functionality broken, prevents character recovery
  3. Combat Panel Does Not Work — Major system failure, needs more details

Medium Priority (UX Issues)

  1. Consume Button Colors — Easy fix, clear solution identified (lines 6246-6247)
  2. Vendor Filter Reset — Frustrating UX issue, needs state persistence
  3. Go Fish Button — Feature appears broken, needs error handling review

Low Priority (Polish/Messages)

  1. HTML Encodings — Display issue, needs standardization audit
  2. Rod Break Message — Incomplete sentence, simple text fix
  3. Vendor Item Counts — Unclear issue, needs clarification

Recommended Investigation Order

  1. Start with #4 (Consume Button Colors) — simplest fix, builds confidence
  2. Add comprehensive logging to #7 (Eating) and #8 (Resting) functions
  3. Test with minimal script configuration to isolate issues
  4. Address #9 (Combat Panel) once more details are available
  5. Fix UX issues (#5, #6) after core functionality is restored
  6. Polish with #2, #7, and #8 as final cleanup

Testing Recommendations

After implementing fixes:

  1. Consume System: Test each consume type (food, drink, booze, potion) with valid items
  2. Resting: Test both short and long rest with various character states
  3. Vendor System: Test purchase flow with different filters applied
  4. Fishing: Test full fishing cycle including rod breaks and leaderboard
  5. Combat: Document specific test cases once more details are available

Each test should verify:

  • ✓ Action completes successfully
  • ✓ UI updates correctly
  • ✓ State persists after page refresh
  • ✓ Error messages are clear and helpful
  • ✓ Edge cases handled gracefully

QA Bug Analysis - Grimbledee's Testing Report

This document analyzes and documents the bugs reported by Grimbledee during testing of the Roll20 GeneSeed suite.

1. HTML Encodings Issue

Description

Text in the UI is displaying HTML entity codes like &amp;, &lt;, &gt; instead of their intended symbols (&, <, >).

Analysis

Location: Multiple files implement their own escapeHtml() functions with inconsistent behavior:

  • Cauldron.js: Contains a non-functional version that only converts to string without escaping
  • PurseStrings.js: Has two definitions - one that properly replaces special characters, but double-encoding may be happening
  • PlayerSoul.js: Uses escapeHtml() throughout the UI rendering (lines 6239, 6241, 6255, etc.)

Root Cause: The problem appears to be double-encoding or decode/encode cycle issues. When text is encoded multiple times (e.g., & → &amp; → &amp;amp;), or when already-encoded text is displayed raw, users see the entity codes instead of symbols.

Affected Areas:

  • Vendor UIs (PurseStrings)
  • PlayerSoul dashboard panels
  • Any HTML output using the inconsistent escapeHtml functions

Suggested Approach

  1. Audit all escapeHtml() function definitions across the codebase
  2. Standardize on a single, canonical implementation (likely in CoreDNA.js or CoreStyles.js)
  3. Add a corresponding unescapeHtml() or decodeHtml() function for reading stored data
  4. Ensure data is encoded exactly once when storing and decoded when reading
  5. Check for double-encoding in vendor/shop HTML generation

2. Number of Items Available for Sale at Vendors

Description

Issue with how the number of items available for sale is displayed or tracked at vendors.

Analysis

Location:

  • PurseStrings.js: Vendor/shop commerce system
  • NpcSoul.js: NPC vendor implementations
  • Related TODO item exists at /TODO/npcsoul-vendor-implementation.md

Current Understanding: The issue description is vague but could relate to:

  • Incorrect inventory counts being displayed
  • Items showing as available when they're not
  • Stock quantities not updating after purchase
  • Display formatting issues in vendor panels

Affected Code:

  • Vendor inventory management in PurseStrings
  • Shop UI rendering in PlayerSoul panels
  • NPC vendor inventory tracking in NpcSoul

Suggested Approach

  1. Review the vendor inventory display logic in PurseStrings
  2. Check if inventory counts are properly decremented after purchases
  3. Verify the NpcSoul vendor implementation matches expected behavior
  4. Add logging to track inventory state changes during purchases
  5. Review the TODO document for known vendor implementation gaps

3. Vendor Filter Reset on Purchase

Description

When buying from a vendor with a filter applied (e.g., "consumables" filter), completing the purchase resets the filter back to "All" instead of maintaining the active filter.

Analysis

Location: PurseStrings.js - vendor buy/sell transaction handlers

Root Cause: The purchase transaction likely rebuilds or refreshes the vendor UI without preserving the current filter state. The filter is probably stored in a local variable or UI state that gets cleared when the panel is regenerated after a successful purchase.

Expected Behavior: After purchasing an item, the vendor panel should:

  1. Update the inventory/stock counts
  2. Refresh the displayed items
  3. Maintain the currently active filter setting
  4. Keep the user's view context (scroll position, filter, etc.)

Affected Flow:

User applies filter → Views filtered items → Clicks buy → Transaction completes → 
Panel refreshes → Filter state lost → Shows "All" items

Suggested Approach

  1. Add filter state persistence to the vendor panel state
  2. Pass current filter as a parameter when regenerating the UI after purchase
  3. Consider using CoreDNA state management to persist filter selection
  4. Add filter state to the URL/command parameters for the vendor panel refresh
  5. Test that filter survives both successful and failed purchase attempts

4. Consume Button Text Color Inconsistency

Description

In the Overview panel's Consume section, button text colors are inconsistent:

  • Eat(1): GREEN when food available ✓ (correct)
  • Drink(0): GRAY when nothing to drink ✓ (correct)
  • Booze(1): WHITE when booze available ✗ (should be green)
  • Potion(2): WHITE when potions available ✗ (should be green)

Analysis

Location: PlayerSoul.js lines 6237-6248

Code Examination:

const linkOrDisabled = (label, href, enabled, primary) => {
    if (!enabled) {
        return `<span ${primary ? actionBtnPrimaryDisabledAttr : actionBtnDisabledAttr}>${escapeHtml(label)}</span>`;
    }
    return `<a ${primary ? actionBtnPrimaryAttr : actionBtnAttr} href="${escapeAttr(href)}">${escapeHtml(label)}</a>`;
};
const consumeButtons = [
    linkOrDisabled(`Eat(${allFood.length})`, eatHref, allFood.length > 0, true),      // primary=true ✓
    linkOrDisabled(`Drink(${allDrink.length})`, drinkHref, allDrink.length > 0, false),  // primary=false
    linkOrDisabled(`Booze(${allBooze.length})`, boozeHref, allBooze.length > 0, false),  // primary=false ✗
    linkOrDisabled(`Potion(${allPotions.length})`, potionHref, allPotions.length > 0, false), // primary=false ✗
    linkOrDisabled('Rest', restHref, true, false)
];

Root Cause: The primary parameter (4th argument) is set to false for Booze and Potion buttons. When items are available, they should use the primary (green) styling to indicate availability, matching the Eat button behavior.

Impact: This inconsistency confuses users about which actions are available. All enabled consume buttons should have the same visual treatment.

Suggested Approach

Simple Fix: Change lines 6246-6247 to:

linkOrDisabled(`Booze(${allBooze.length})`, boozeHref, allBooze.length > 0, true),   // primary=true
linkOrDisabled(`Potion(${allPotions.length})`, potionHref, allPotions.length > 0, true), // primary=true

This makes all consume buttons use primary (green) styling when items are available, creating visual consistency across the interface.

5. Rod Break Message - Incomplete Sentence

Description

Fishing rod break message shows: "Your rod snapped while fighting a large." - sentence appears incomplete.

Analysis

Location: GoFish!.js line 1513

Current Code:

entry.stats.heroHtml = `<div style="padding:8px; border:1px solid #8a2c2c; border-radius:8px; background:#3a1f1f; color:#ffd1d1;"><strong>💥 Rod Break</strong><div style="font-size:12px; margin-top:4px;">Your rod snapped while fighting a ${data.fishSize || 'fish'}.</div></div>`;

Root Cause: The template uses data.fishSize which might contain just an adjective like "large" instead of a complete noun phrase like "large fish". When fishSize is a descriptor word, the sentence reads "...fighting a large." instead of "...fighting a large fish."

Expected: "Your rod snapped while fighting a large fish."

Suggested Approach

Option 1 - Simple Fix: Always append "fish" to ensure grammatical completeness:

`Your rod snapped while fighting a ${data.fishSize || 'large'} fish.`

Option 2 - Context-Aware: Check if fishSize is a complete phrase or just an adjective:

const fishDescription = data.fishSize && data.fishSize.includes('fish') 
    ? data.fishSize 
    : `${data.fishSize || 'large'} fish`;
`Your rod snapped while fighting a ${fishDescription}.`

Option 3 - Use Fish Name: If available, use the actual fish name:

`Your rod snapped while fighting a ${data.fishName || 'large fish'}.`

6. Fishing Leaderboard - "Go Fish" Button Does Nothing

Description

From the Fishing Leaderboard panel in PlayerSoul, clicking the "Go Fish" pill plays the fishing sound but no visible action occurs.

Analysis

Location: GoFish!.js lines 1599, 4567-4629

Button HTML (line 1599):

`<a style="${goFishBtnStyle}" href="!go-fish refish --charid|${charId} --poolid|${poolId}">
    <span>${goFishIcon}</span><span>${goFishLabel}</span>
</a>`

Command Handler (lines 4567-4629):

} else if (actionCommand === 'refish') {
    // ... validation code ...
    const refishToken = findObjs({ _type: 'graphic', represents: cid, layer: 'objects' })[0];
    if (!refishToken) {
        throw new Error("[Invalid] No active token found for refish.");
    }
    // ... more code ...
    GoFish.Panel.StartSessionForTokenWithMeta(refishToken, `Fishing at ${arg1}/${arg2}`, { poolId, source: arg1, level: arg2 }, { activate: true, quiet: true });
    fisher.goFishing(refishToken, arg1, arg2, toWhom);
}

Root Cause Theories:

  1. Token Not Found: The findObjs call for the token might fail if the character token isn't on the objects layer or is on a different page
  2. Silent Failure: The { quiet: true } option might suppress error messages, causing failures to be invisible
  3. Sound Plays But Logic Fails: Sound effect triggers but the actual fishing session fails to start
  4. Panel Not Refreshing: The fishing UI panel doesn't update to show the new session
  5. Missing Panel Context: Leaderboard view might not have the necessary context to start a new fishing session

Suggested Approach

  1. Add comprehensive logging to the refish command handler
  2. Remove or conditionally use { quiet: true } to allow error messages during debugging
  3. Validate token existence and state before attempting to fish
  4. Check if GoFish.Panel.RefreshIfVisible() is being called after the fishing action
  5. Verify that the leaderboard has access to the required poolId and location data
  6. Add user-facing error messages when fishing cannot start from the leaderboard
  7. Consider if the leaderboard should redirect to the main fishing panel instead

7. Eating Does Not Eat

Description

The eating functionality is not working - consuming food items does not actually consume them.

Analysis

Location: PlayerSoul.js function consumeItem starting at line 9545

Current Implementation:
The consumeItem function is extensive (lines 9545-9900+) and handles:

  • Item lookup via PurseStrings.getCheckObjectByName(consumeable) (line 9550)
  • Parsing item modifiers (protien, hydration, buffs, etc.) starting at line 9599
  • Applying effects to the soul object (lines 9600-9800+)

Problem Areas:

  1. Item Removal: Need to verify that items are actually removed from inventory after consumption
  2. Transaction Commit: Changes might not be persisted to state
  3. Observer Notification: The notifyObservers('soulChange', soul, deepCopy(soul)) might not be triggered
  4. PurseStrings Integration: The item might not be removed from the character's inventory in the PurseStrings system
  5. Error Handling: Silent failures might prevent consumption without user feedback

Expected Flow:

User clicks "Eat" → Select item → consumeItem called → 
Parse item effects → Apply to soul → Remove from inventory → 
Update UI → Notify observers → Show success message

Suggested Approach

  1. Add detailed logging at each step of the consumeItem function
  2. Verify that PurseStrings.removeItemFromInventory() or equivalent is being called
  3. Check that changes to the soul object are being saved to state
  4. Ensure notifyObservers is called after successful consumption
  5. Verify the item query/dropdown is passing the correct item name
  6. Check for case-sensitivity or name-matching issues in item lookup
  7. Add user feedback (success/failure messages) at the end of consumption
  8. Test with A5edDB.GetDB() validation to ensure items are valid

8. Resting Does Not Rest

Description

Long rest functionality is not working - taking a long rest does not update the character's rest status.

Analysis

Location: PlayerSoul.js function consumeRest starting at line 9189

Current Implementation:

function consumeRest(soul, message, restType) {
    // ... validation and setup ...
    
    switch (restType) {
        case LONG_REST_COMMAND:
            if (soul.insomnia) {
                // Block rest if insomnia
                return;
            }
            applyRestToSoul(RestInStyle.LongRest);  // <-- Delegates to RestInStyle
            soul.fatigue = FULLY_RESTED;             // <-- Updates soul state
            // ... fishing rod repair code ...
            notifyObservers('soulChange', soul, deepCopy(soul));  // <-- Notify
            // ... more code ...
    }
}

Integration Points:

  • Uses 5E Resting in Style.js via RestInStyle.LongRest (line 9227)
  • Updates soul.fatigue directly (line 9228)
  • Repairs fishing rods as a side effect (lines 9230-9248)
  • Calls notifyObservers to propagate changes (line 9249)

Problem Areas:

  1. RestInStyle Undefined: The RestInStyle script might not be loaded or the function might not exist
  2. Character Selection: message.selected might not contain the correct token reference
  3. State Not Persisting: Changes to soul.fatigue might not be saved back to state
  4. Observer Not Triggering: UI might not refresh after rest completes
  5. Error Swallowing: Multiple try/catch blocks with // ignore comments might hide critical errors

Expected Behavior:

  • Character HP restored
  • Hit dice reset/available
  • Spell slots restored
  • soul.fatigue set to FULLY_RESTED (1440)
  • Fishing rod durability restored to max
  • UI updated to show rested state

Suggested Approach

  1. Verify 5E Resting in Style.js is loaded and RestInStyle.LongRest exists
  2. Add error logging instead of silently ignoring exceptions
  3. Check that message.selected contains valid token objects
  4. Verify state changes are persisted via CoreDNA state management
  5. Ensure observer notifications trigger UI refresh
  6. Test with and without tokens selected
  7. Add explicit success/failure feedback to the user
  8. Check character sheet attribute updates (hp_current, spell slots, etc.)

9. Combat Panel Does Not Work

Description

The combat panel is not functioning properly.

Analysis

Location:

  • Combat Master.js: Main combat system (276,569 bytes)
  • Mass Combat.js: Large-scale combat system (72,505 bytes)
  • RoundMaster.js: Turn tracking and round management (290,916 bytes)
  • PlayerSoul.js: May have combat panel UI integration

Insufficient Information: The bug report doesn't specify:

  • What "does not work" means (crashes, doesn't display, buttons don't work, etc.)
  • Which combat panel is affected (Combat Master, Mass Combat, or PlayerSoul combat UI)
  • What error messages appear, if any
  • What actions fail (starting combat, ending turns, tracking initiative, etc.)

Potential Issues:

  1. Script Load Order: Combat scripts might depend on other scripts not loaded yet
  2. Command Handler: Combat command parsing might be broken
  3. UI Rendering: Panel HTML generation might have errors
  4. State Management: Combat state might not be initializing properly
  5. Integration: Links from PlayerSoul to combat systems might be broken
  6. Dependencies: Missing required libraries or functions

Suggested Approach

  1. Gather More Information:

    • What specific action is attempted?
    • Are there any error messages in the API console?
    • Which combat system is being used?
    • Does the panel display at all, or fail to render?

  2. Debugging Steps:

    • Check the API console for JavaScript errors
    • Verify all combat scripts are loaded in correct order
    • Test basic commands like !combat help or equivalent
    • Check if CoreDNA and dependencies are loaded before combat scripts
    • Review script startup logs for initialization errors

  3. Common Combat Issues:

    • Token selection problems
    • Initiative tracking failures
    • Turn order display issues
    • Effect/status marker management
    • Integration with character sheets

  4. Next Steps:

    • Request detailed reproduction steps from Grimbledee
    • Collect API console logs during the failure
    • Test in isolation with minimal script load
    • Check for conflicts with other combat-related scripts

Summary of Findings

High Priority (Blocking User Actions)

  1. Eating Does Not Eat - Core functionality broken, prevents item consumption
  2. Resting Does Not Rest - Core functionality broken, prevents character recovery
  3. Combat Panel Does Not Work - Major system failure, needs more details

Medium Priority (UX Issues)

  1. Consume Button Colors - Easy fix, clear solution identified (lines 6246-6247)
  2. Vendor Filter Reset - Frustrating UX issue, needs state persistence
  3. Go Fish Button - Feature appears broken, needs error handling review

Low Priority (Polish/Messages)

  1. HTML Encodings - Display issue, needs standardization audit
  2. Rod Break Message - Incomplete sentence, simple text fix
  3. Vendor Item Counts - Unclear issue, needs clarification

Recommended Investigation Order

  1. Start with #4 (Consume Button Colors) - simplest fix, builds confidence
  2. Add comprehensive logging to #7 (Eating) and #8 (Resting) functions
  3. Test with minimal script configuration to isolate issues
  4. Address #9 (Combat Panel) once more details are available
  5. Fix UX issues (#5, #6) after core functionality is restored
  6. Polish with #2, #7, and #8 as final cleanup

Testing Recommendations

After implementing fixes:

  1. Consume System: Test each consume type (food, drink, booze, potion) with valid items
  2. Resting: Test both short and long rest with various character states
  3. Vendor System: Test purchase flow with different filters applied
  4. Fishing: Test full fishing cycle including rod breaks and leaderboard
  5. Combat: Document specific test cases once more details are available

Each test should verify:

  • ✓ Action completes successfully
  • ✓ UI updates correctly
  • ✓ State persists after page refresh
  • ✓ Error messages are clear and helpful
  • ✓ Edge cases handled gracefully
